name: Charity shall cover a multitude of sins
notes: Solomon's proverb that 'love covereth all sins' (Prov 10:12) is taken up by two apostles writing of the same grace. Peter exhorts fervent charity 'for charity shall cover the multitude of sins' (1Pet 4:8), and James says he who turns a sinner from his way 'shall hide a multitude of sins' (Jas 5:20). The shared core is the covering or hiding of sins by love. Peter and James share the near-identical phrase 'multitude of sins,' the apostolic echo of the proverb's 'all sins.' Each keeps its own vantage: Solomon contrasts love with strife-stirring hatred, Peter grounds the covering in mutual charity, and James in the saving of a soul from death.
